name: Espresso Fudge Cups
recipeIngredient: Instant espresso or coffee granules (1 tsp)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. Line twenty-four 1-1/2 inch miniature muffin cups with paper liners.
3. To make batter: Place butter and chocolate in a medium microwavesafe bowl and microwave at 60% power for 1 to 2 minutes, or until smooth. Alternatively, melt butter and chocolate in a heavy small saucepan over low heat.
4. Remove from heat and whisk in eggs. Stir in flour, coffee powder, and sugar, then mix well. Add chopped pecans and spoon into muffin papers. If not glazing the cups, top each with a pecan half.
5. Bake for 17 to 20 minutes, or until the tops are set. Do not overbake; they should be fudgy instead of dry.
6. Cool for 2 to 3 minutes before removing from racks to cool completely.
7. To make chocolate glaze: In a 4-cup microwavesafe bowl, melt chocolate, butter, corn syrup, coffee, and Kahlua on high (100%), uncovered, for 1 to 3 minutes, stirring every minute until smooth. Alternatively, melt in a heavy saucepan over low heat, stirring until smooth.
8. If too thick, thin with additional Kahlua. Dip tops of cups into warm glaze and garnish with frosting decorations.
9. Cups may be stored airtight at room temperature up to 2 days or frozen up to 1 month. Defrost, covered, in one layer at room temperature.
Nutritional Analysis: Per fudge cup (excluding glaze): 114 calories, 8 grams fat, 28 mg cholesterol, 47 mg sodium.
